reason: Response A develops a single concrete idea (the half-second lag of conscious experience) with precise, vivid detail and a controlled arc that turns the limitation into something beautiful, showing the tighter craft and originality typical of Sonnet. Response B leans on a well-known reference (Hofstadter's strange loop) and restates the same self-reference point in successive paragraphs with more generic, repetitive phrasing, which reads more like Haiku's surface-level treatment. The greater conceptual specificity and structural discipline in A point to the more capable model.
